model_info.name: LightGBM Classifier
model_info.description: Gradient Boosting classifier trained on elephant-specific acoustic fingerprints

fingerprint_blocks.name: Rumble Energy
fingerprint_blocks.description: Detailed sub-bands capturing infrasonic structure unique to elephant vocalizations

fingerprint_blocks.name: Call Tremor
fingerprint_blocks.description: Biological vibration pattern from the elephant's larynx during vocalization

fingerprint_blocks.name: Temporal Phases
fingerprint_blocks.description: Onset, body, and offset patterns showing how calls fade in and out

fingerprint_blocks.name: Vocal Timbre
fingerprint_blocks.description: Voice texture differentiating call types like Social vs Alarm rumbles

key_insights.title: How calls END matters more than how they START
key_insights.description: The rate of decay (how quickly the call fades) is the #1 predictor of context. Alarm calls end abruptly while social calls taper gently.

key_insights.title: The 7.7 Hz 'tremor' is a biological signature
key_insights.description: Elephants produce a characteristic vocal tremor caused by laryngeal oscillation. Its intensity varies by emotional state.

key_insights.title: Infrasonic ratios reveal intent
key_insights.description: The balance between deep infrasound (8-120 Hz) and higher harmonics distinguishes contact calls from distress signals.
